title: Navigating Legacy Mixed-Signal Upgrades: Modernizing Designs Around the AD7225LRZ Quad DAC
gist: A forum member opens a technical discussion (not a report) asking the SemiWiki community for design guidance on migrating away from Analog Devices' legacy parallel-bus AD7225LRZ quad DAC, covering latch-up/power-sequencing risk, parallel-to-serial interface bridging, and the scarcity of pin-compatible modern replacements.

insights.claim: The AD7225LRZ quad 8-bit DAC, built on Analog Devices' proprietary LC²MOS process, is exposed to destructive parasitic SCR latch-up if analog supply or reference voltage rails ramp up out of sequence relative to digital inputs, a particular risk in ruggedized industrial or defense systems with transient power dropouts.
insights.speaker: P
insights.so_what: Highlights a concrete failure mode (SCR latch-up from supply-sequencing errors) that system integrators using legacy CMOS mixed-signal ICs in defense/industrial designs must actively protect against.
insights.quote: If the analog supplies ($V_{DD}$, $V_{SS}$) or the reference voltages ($V_{REF}$) ramp up in the wrong order relative to the digital inputs, parasitic SCR latch-up can occur, leading to destructive current draw.
insights.locator: The Latch-Up and Power Sequencing Conundrum

insights.claim: Modern MCUs and FPGAs have largely moved to serial interfaces (SPI, I2C), making the AD7225's 8-bit parallel bus plus address and control lines a 12+ pin GPIO burden that complicates digital-controller upgrades while keeping the legacy analog stage unchanged.
insights.speaker: P
insights.so_what: Illustrates a structural mismatch between legacy parallel-interface analog ICs and the low-pin-count serial ecosystem of current MCUs/FPGAs, a recurring cost driver for board redesigns in long-lifecycle defense/industrial programs.
insights.quote: supporting the AD7225's 8-bit parallel bus plus address lines ($A0, A1$) and control signals ($\overline{WR}$, $\overline{LDAC}$) requires significant GPIO overhead (often up to 12+ pins)
insights.locator: Parallel vs. Serial: Microprocessor Interface Bottlenecks

insights.claim: Most modern quad 8-bit or 10-bit DACs have migrated entirely to TSSOP/QFN packages with SPI interfaces, leaving the AD7225LRZ's SOIC-24 parallel footprint without a true pin-compatible drop-in replacement, creating supply-chain and long-term obsolescence risk for legacy designs.
insights.speaker: P
insights.so_what: Signals a component-level obsolescence gap in the analog IC market that could sustain demand for interposer/bridge solutions or drive costly full board redesigns for defense/aerospace programs still using legacy parallel DACs.
insights.quote: Most modern quad 8-bit or 10-bit DACs have migrated entirely to TSSOP/QFN packages with SPI interfaces, leaving the SOIC-24 parallel footprint behind.
insights.locator: Finding True Drop-In or Near-Equivalent Alternatives
